author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: 1 John II. 27–III. 8.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “And it is true, and lieth not.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Even as it hath taught you, abide in it.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And now, little children, abide in Him; that, when He shall appear, we may have confidence, and not be put to shame by Him at His coming.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: If ye know that He is righteous, know ye that every one that doeth righteousness is born of Him.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Behold, what manner of love the Father hath bestowed upon us, that we should be called and should be the sons of God: therefore the world knoweth us not, because it knew not Him, us also the world knoweth not.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Beloved, now are we the sons of God, and it is not yet manifested what we shall be.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: We know that, when He shall appear, we shall be like Him; for we shall see Him as He is.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And every man that hath this hope in Him purifieth himself, even as He is pure.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Whosoever committeth sin committeth also iniquity.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Sin is iniquity.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And ye know that He was manifested to take away sin; and in Him is no sin.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Whosoever abideth in Him sinneth not: whosoever sinneth hath not seen Him, neither known Him.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Little children, let no man seduce you.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He that doeth righteousness is righteous, even as He is righteous.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He that committeth sin is of the devil; for the devil sinneth from the beginning.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For this purpose the Son of God was manifested; that He might destroy the works of the devil.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Ye remember, brethren, that yesterday’s lesson was brought to a close at this point, that “ye have no need that any man teach you, but the unction itself teacheth you concerning all things.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Now this, as I am sure ye remember, we so expounded to you, that we who from without speak to your ears, are as workmen applying culture from without to a tree, but we cannot give the increase nor form the fruits: but only He that created and redeemed and called you, He, dwelling in you by faith and the Spirit, must speak to you within, else vain is all our noise of words.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Whence does this appear?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: From this: that while many hear, not all are persuaded of that which is said, but only they to whom God speaks within.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Now they to whom He speaks within, are those who give place to Him: and those give place to God, who “give not place to the devil.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For the devil wishes to inhabit the hearts of men, and speak there the things which are able to seduce.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But what saith the Lord Jesus?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “The prince of this world is cast out.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Whence cast? out of heaven and earth? out of the fabric of the world?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Nay, but out of the hearts of the believing.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: The invader being cast out, let the Redeemer dwell within: because the same redeemed, who created.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And the devil now assaults from without, not conquers Him that hath possession within.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And he assaults from without, by casting in various temptations: but that person consents not thereto, to whom God speaks within, and the unction of which ye have heard.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “And it is true,” namely, this same unction; i.e. the very Spirit of the Lord which teacheth men, cannot lie: “and is not false.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Even as it hath taught you, abide ye in the same.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And now, little children, abide ye in Him, that when He shall be manifested, we may have boldness in His sight, that we be not put to shame by Him at His coming.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Ye see, brethren: we believe on Jesus whom we have not seen: they announced Him, that saw, that handled, that heard the word out of His own mouth; and that they might persuade all mankind of the truth thereof, they were sent by Him, not dared to go of themselves.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And whither were they sent?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Ye heard while the Gospel was read, “Go, preach the Gospel to the whole creation which is under heaven.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Consequently, the disciples were sent “every where:” with signs and wonders to attest that what they spake, they had seen.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And we believe on Him whom we have not seen, and we look for Him to come.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Whoso look for Him by faith, shall rejoice when He cometh: those who are without faith, when that which now they see not is come, shall be ashamed.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And that confusion of face shall not be for a single day and so pass away, in such sort as those are wont to be confounded, who are found out in some fault, and are scoffed at by their fellowmen.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: That confusion shall carry them that are confounded to the left hand, that to them it may be said, “Go into everlasting fire, prepared for the devil and his angels.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Let us abide then in His words, that we be not confounded when He cometh.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For Himself saith in the Gospel to them that had believed on Him: “If ye shall abide in my word, then are ye verily my disciples.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And, as if they had asked, With what fruit?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “And,” saith He, “ye shall know the truth, and the truth shall make you free.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For as yet our salvation is in hope, not in deed: for we do not already possess that which is promised, but we hope for it to come.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And “faithful is He that promised;” He deceiveth not thee: only do thou not faint, but wait for the promise.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For He, the Truth, cannot deceive.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Be not thou a liar, to profess one thing and do another; keep thou the faith, and He keeps His promise.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But if thou keep not the faith, thine own self, not He that promised, hath defrauded thee.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “If ye know that He is righteous, know ye that every one that doeth righteousness is born of Him.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: The righteousness which at present is ours is of faith.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Perfect righteousness is not, save only in the angels: and scarce in angels, if they be compared with God: yet if there be any perfect righteousness of souls and spirits which God hath created, it is in the angels, holy, just, good, by no lapse turned aside, by no pride falling, but remaining ever in the contemplation of the Word of God, and having nothing else sweet unto them save Him by whom they were created; in them is perfect righteousness: but in us it has begun to be, of faith, by the Spirit.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Ye heard when the Psalm was read, “Begin ye to the Lord in confession.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “Begin,” saith it; the beginning of our righteousness is the confession of sins.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Thou hast begun not to defend thy sin; now hast thou made a beginning of righteousness: but it shall be perfected in thee when to do nothing else shall delight thee, when “death shall be swallowed up in victory,” when there shall be no itching of lust, when there shall be no struggling with flesh and blood, when there shall be the palm of victory, the triumph over the enemy; then shall there be perfect righteousness.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: At present we are still fighting: if we fight we are in the lists; we smite and are smitten; but who shall conquer, remains to be seen.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And that man conquers, who even when he smites presumes not on his own strength, but relies upon God that cheers him on.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: The devil is alone when he fights against us.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: If we are with God, we overcome the devil: for if thou fight alone with the devil, thou wilt be overcome.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He is a skillful enemy: how may palms has he won!

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Consider to what he has cast us down!

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: That we are born mortal, comes of this, that he in the first place cast down from Paradise our very original.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: What then is to be done, seeing he is so well practised?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Let the Almighty be invoked to thine aid against the devices of the devil.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Let Him dwell in thee, who cannot be overcome, and thou shalt securely overcome him who is wont to overcome.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But to overcome whom?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Those in whom God dwelleth not.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For, that ye may know it, brethren; Adam being in Paradise despised the commandment of God, and lifted up the neck, as if he desired to be his own master, and were loath to be subject to the will of God: so he fell from that immortality, from that blessedness.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But there was a certain man, a man now well skilled, though a mortal born, who even as he sat on the dunghill, putrifying with worms, overcame the devil: yea, Adam himself then overcame: even he, in Job; because Job was of his race.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: So then, Adam, overcome in Paradise, overcame on the dunghill.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Being in Paradise, he gave ear to the persuasion of the woman which the devil had put into her: but being on the dunghill he said to Eve, “Thou hast spoken as one of the foolish women.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: There he lent an ear, here he gave an answer: when he was glad, he listened, when he was scourged, he overcame.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Therefore, see what follows, my brethren, in the Epistle: because this is what it would have us lay to heart, that we may overcome the devil indeed, but not of ourselves.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “If ye know that He is righteous,” saith it, “know ye that every one that doeth righteousness is born of Him:” of God, of Christ.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And in that he hath said, “Is born of Him,” he cheers us on.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Already therefore, in that we are born of Him, we are perfect.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “Behold what manner of love the Father hath given us, that we should be called sons of God, and be (such).”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For whoso are called sons, and are not sons, what profiteth them the name where the thing is not?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: How many are called physicians, who know not how to heal! how many are called watchers, who sleep all night long!

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: So, many are called Christians, and yet in deeds are not found such; because they are not this which they are called, that is, in life, in manners, in faith, in hope, in charity.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But what have ye heard here, brethren?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “Behold, what manner of love the Father hath bestowed upon us, that we should be called, and should be, the sons of God: therefore the world knoweth us not, because it hath not known Him, us also the world knoweth not.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: There is a whole world Christian, and a whole world ungodly; because throughout the whole world there are ungodly, and throughout the whole world there are godly: those know not these.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: In what sense, think we, do they not know them?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: They deride them that live good lives.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Mark well and see: for haply there are such also among you.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Each one of you who now lives godly, who despises worldly things, who does not choose to go to spectacles, who does not choose to make himself drunken as it were by solemn custom, yea, what is worse, under countenance of holy days to make himself unclean: the man who does not choose to do these things, how is he derided by those who do them!

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Would he be scoffed at if he were known?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But why is he not known?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “The world knoweth Him not.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Who is “the world”?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Those inhabiters of the world.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Just as we say, “a house;” meaning, its inhabitants.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: These things have been said to you again and again, and we forbear to repeat them to your disgust.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: By this time, when ye hear the word “world,” in a bad signification, ye know that ye must understand it to mean only lovers of the world because through love they inhabit, and by inhabiting have become entitled to the name.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Therefore the world hath not known us, because it hath not known Him.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He walked here Himself, the Lord Jesus Christ in the flesh; He was God, He was latent in weakness.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And wherefore was He not known?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Because He reproved all sins in men.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: They, through loving the delights of sins, did not acknowledge the God: through loving that which the fever prompted, they did wrong to the Physician.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For us then, what are we?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Already we are begotten of Him; but because we are such in hope, he saith, “Beloved, now are we sons of God.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Now already?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Then what is it we look for, if already we are sons of God?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “And not yet,” saith he, “is it manifested what we shall be.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But what else shall we be than sons of God?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Hear what follows: “We know that, when He shall appear, we shall be like Him, because we shall see Him as He is.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Understand, my beloved.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: It is a great matter: “We know that, when He shall appear, we shall be like Him; for we shall see Him as He is.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: In the first place mark, what is called “Is.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Ye know what it is that is so called.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: That which is called “Is,” and not only is called but is so, is unchangeable: It ever remaineth, It cannot be changed, It is in no part corruptible: It hath neither proficiency, for It is perfect; nor hath deficiency, for It is eternal.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And what is this?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And what is this?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “Who being in the form of God, thought it not robbery to be equal with God.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: To see Christ in this sort, Christ in the form of God, Word of God, Only-Begotten of the Father, equal with the Father, is to the bad impossible.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But in regard that the Word was made flesh, the bad also shall have power to see Him: because in the day of judgment the bad also will see Him; for He shall so come to judge, as He came to be judged.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: In the selfsame form, a man, but yet God: for “cursed is every one that putteth his trust in man.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: A man, He came to be judged, a man, He will come to judge.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And if He shall not be seen, what is this that is written, “They shall look on Him whom they pierced?”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For of the ungodly it is said, that they shall see and be confounded.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: How shall the ungodly not see, when He shall set some on the right hand, others on the left?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: To those on the right hand He will say, “Come, ye blessed of my Father, receive the kingdom:” to those on the left He will say, “Go into everlasting fire.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: They will see but the form of a servant, the form of God they will not see.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Why? because they were ungodly; and the Lord Himself saith, “Blessed are the pure in heart, for they shall see God.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Therefore, we are to see a certain vision, my brethren, “which neither eye hath seen, nor ear hath heard, nor hath entered into the heart of man:” a certain vision, a vision surpassing all earthly beautifulness, of gold, of silver, of groves and fields; the beautifulness of sea and air, the beautifulness of sun and moon, the beautifulness of the stars, the beautifulness of angels: surpassing all things: because from it are all things beautiful.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: What then shall “we” be, when we shall see this?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: What is promised to us?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “We shall be like Him, for we shall see Him as He is.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: The tongue hath done what it could, hath sounded the words: let the rest be thought by the heart.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For what hath even John himself said in comparison of That which Is, or what can be said by us men, who are so far from being equal to his merits?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Return we therefore to that unction of Him, return we to that unction which inwardly teacheth that which we cannot speak: and because ye cannot at present see, let your part and duty be in desire.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: The whole life of a good Christian is an holy desire.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Now what thou longest for, thou dost not yet see: howbeit by longing, thou art made capable, so that when that is come which thou mayest see, thou shall be filled.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For just as, if thou wouldest fill a bag, and knowest how great the thing is that shall be given, thou stretchest the opening of the sack or the skin, or whatever else it be; thou knowest how much thou wouldest put in, and seest that the bag is narrow; by stretching thou makest it capable of holding more: so God, by deferring our hope, stretches our desire; by the desiring, stretches the mind; by stretching, makes it more capacious.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Let us desire therefore, my brethren, for we shall be filled.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: See Paul widening, as it were, his bosom, that it may be able to receive that which is to come.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He saith, namely, “Not that I have already received, or am already perfect: brethren, I deem not myself to have apprehended.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Then what art thou doing in this life, if thou have not yet apprehended?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “But this one thing [I do]; forgetting the things that are behind, reaching forth to the things that are before, upon the strain I follow on unto the prize of the high calling.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He says he reaches forth, or stretches himself, and says that he follows “upon the strain.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He felt himself too little to take in that “which eye hath not seen, nor ear heard, neither hath entered into the heart of man.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: This is our life, that by longing we should be exercised.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But holy longing exercises us just so much as we prune off our longings from the love of the world.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: We have already said, “Empty out that which is to be filled.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: With good thou art to be filled: pour out the bad.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Suppose that God would fill thee with honey: if thou art full of vinegar, where wilt thou put the honey?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: That which the vessel bore in it must be poured out: the vessel itself must be cleansed; must be cleansed, albeit with labor, albeit with hard rubbing, that it may become fit for that thing, whatever it be.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Let us say honey, say gold, say wine; whatever we say it is, being that which cannot be said, whatever we would fain say, It is called—God.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And when we say “God,” what have we said?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Is that one syllable the whole of that we look for?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: So then, whatever we have had power to say is beneath Him: let us stretch ourselves unto Him, that when He shall come, He may fill us.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For “we shall be like Him; because we shall see Him as He is.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “And every one that hath this hope in Him.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Ye see how he hath set us our place, in “hope.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Ye see how the Apostle Paul agreeth with his fellow-apostle, “By hope we are saved.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But hope that is seen, is not hope: for what a man seeth, why doth he hope for?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For if what we see not, we hope for, by patience we wait for it.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: This very patience exerciseth desire.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Continue thou, for He continueth: and persevere thou in walking, that thou mayest reach the goal: for that to which thou tendest will not remove.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: See: “And every one that hath this hope in Him, purifieth himself even as He is pure.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: See how he has not taken away free-will, in that he saith, “purifieth himself.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Who purifieth us but God?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Yea, but God doth not purify thee if thou be unwilling.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Therefore, in that thou joinest thy will to God, in that thou purifiest thyself.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Thou purifiest thyself, not by thyself, but by Him who cometh to inhabit thee.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Still, because thou doest somewhat therein by the will, therefore is somewhat attributed to thee.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But it is attributed to thee only to the end thou shouldest say, as in the Psalm, “Be thou my helper, forsake me not.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: If thou sayest, “Be thou my helper,” thou doest somewhat: for if thou be doing nothing, how should He be said to “help” thee?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “Every one that doeth sin, doeth also iniquity.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Let no man say, Sin is one thing, iniquity another: let no man say, I am a sinful man, but not a doer of iniquity.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For, “Every one that doeth sin, doeth also iniquity.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Sin is iniquity.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Well then, what are we to do concerning sins and iniquities?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Hear what He saith: “And ye know that He was manifested to take away sin; and sin in Him is not.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He, in Whom sin is not, the same is come to take away sin.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For were there sin in Him, it must be taken away from Him, not He take it away Himself.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “Whosoever abideth in Him, sinneth not.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: In so far as he abideth in Him, in so far sinneth not.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “Whosoever sinneth hath not seen Him, neither known Him.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: A great question this: “Whosoever sinneth hath not seen Him, neither known Him.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: We have not seen Him, but are to see; have not known Him, but are to know: we believe on One we have not known.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Or haply, by faith we have known, and by actual beholding have not yet known?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But then in faith we have both seen and known.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For if faith doth not yet see, why are we said to have been enlightened?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: There is an enlightening by faith, and an enlightening by sight.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: At present, while we are on pilgrimage, “we walk by faith, not by sight,” or, actually beholding.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Therefore also our righteousness is “by faith, not by sight.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Our righteousness shall be perfect, when we shall see by actual beholding.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Only, in the meanwhile, let us not leave that righteousness which is of faith, since “the just doth live by faith,” as saith the apostle.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “Whosoever abideth in Him, sinneth not.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For, “whosoever sinneth, hath not seen Him, neither known Him.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: That man who sins, believes not: but if a man believes, so far as pertains to his faith, he sinneth not.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “Little children, let no man seduce you.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He that doeth righteousness is righteous, as He is righteous.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: What, on hearing that we are “righteous as He is righteous,” are we to think ourselves equal with God?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Ye must know what means that “as:” thus he said a while ago, “Purifieth himself even as He is pure.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Then is our purity like and equal to the purity of God, and our righteousness to God’s righteousness?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Who can say this?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But the word “as,” is not always wont to be used in the sense of equality.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: As, for example, if, having seen this large church, a person should wish to build a smaller church, but with the same relative dimensions: as, for example, if this be one measure in width and two measures in length, he too should build his church one measure in width and two measures in length: in that case one sees that he has built it “as” this is built.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But this church has, say, a hundred cubits in length, the other thirty: it is at once “as” this, and yet unequal.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Ye see that this “as” is not always referred to parity and equality.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For example, see what a difference there is between the face of a man and its image from a mirror: there is a face in the image, a face in the body: the image exists in imitation, the body in reality.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And what do we say?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Why, “as” there are eyes here, so also there; “as” ears here, so ears also there.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: The thing is different, but the “as” is said of the resemblance.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Well then, we also have in us the image of God; but not that which the Son equal with the Father hath: yet except we also, according to our measure, were “as” He, we should in no respect be said to be like Him.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “He purifieth us,” then, “even as He is pure:” but He is pure from eternity, we pure by faith.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: We are “righteous even as He is righteous;” but He is so in His immutable perpetuity, we righteous by believing on One we do not see, that so we may one day see Him.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Even when our righteousness shall be perfect, when we shall be equal to the angels, not even then shall it be equalled with Him.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: How far then is it from Him now, when not even then it shall be equal!

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “He that doeth sin, is of the devil, because the devil sinneth from the beginning.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “Is of the devil:” ye know what he means: by imitating the devil.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For the devil made no man, begat no man, created no man: but whoso imitates the devil, that person, as if begotten of him, becomes a child of the devil; by imitating him, not literally by being begotten of him.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: In what sense art thou a child of Abraham, not that Abraham begat thee?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: In the same sense as the Jews, the children of Abraham, not imitating the faith of Abraham, are become children of the devil: of the flesh of Abraham they were begotten, and the faith of Abraham they have not imitated.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: If then those who were thence begotten were put out of the inheritance, because they did not imitate, thou, who art not begotten of him, art made a child, and in this way shall be a child of him by imitating him.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And if thou imitate the devil, in such wise as he became proud and impious against God, thou wilt be a child of the devil: by imitating, not that he created thee or begat thee.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “Unto this end was the Son of God manifested.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Now then, brethren, mark!

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: All sinners are begotten of the devil, as sinners.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Adam was made by God: but when he consented to the devil, he was begotten of the devil; and he begat all men such as he was himself.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: With lust itself we were born; even before we add our sins, from that condemnation we have our birth.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For if we are born without any sin, wherefore this running with infants to baptism that they may be released?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Then mark well, brethren, the two birth-stocks, Adam and Christ: two men are; but one of them, a man that is man; the other, a Man that is God.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: By the man that is man we are sinners; by the Man that is God we are justified.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: That birth hath cast down unto death; this birth hath raised up unto life: that birth brings with it sin; this birth setteth free from sin.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For to this end came Christ as Man, to undo the sins of men.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “Unto this end was the Son of God manifested, that He may undo the works of the devil.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: The rest I commend to your thoughts, my beloved, that I may not burden you.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For the question we labor to solve is even this—that we call ourselves sinners: for if any man shall say that he is without sin, he is a liar.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And in the Epistle of this same John we have found it written, “If we say that we have no sin, we deceive ourselves.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For ye should remember what went before: “If we say that we have no sin, we deceive ourselves, and the truth is not in us.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And yet, on the other hand, in what follows thou art told, “He that is begotten of God sinneth not: he that doeth sin hath not seen Him, neither known Him.—Every one that doeth sin is of the devil:” sin is not of God: this affrights us again.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: In what sense are we begotten of God, and in what sense do we confess ourselves sinners?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Shall we say, because we are not begotten of God?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And what do these Sacraments in regard to infants?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: What hath John said?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “He that is begotten of God, sinneth not.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And yet again the same John hath said, “If we say that we have no sin, we deceive ourselves, and the truth is not in us!”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: A great question it is, and an embarrassing one; and may I have made you intent upon having it solved, my beloved.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Tomorrow, in the name of the Lord, what He will give, we will discourse thereof.
